question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method reads the latest record file from the database at the start of execution but does not re-validate it during the iteration, allowing a concurrent importer run to add new record files that shift the pruning boundary mid-execution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd()) query to perform a full table scan on a very large record_file table (due to missing index on consensus_end), causing the query to time out and the prune() method to throw an exception that halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method does not check the health of the database connection before starting, causing it to attempt pruning on a degraded connection that holds partial locks and blocks the importer's transaction insertion path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the entity_history table pruning (via EntityHistoryRepository.prune()) to delete entity state records that the importer needs to resolve account IDs for new incoming transactions, causing the importer to fail entity resolution and halt transaction processing?
